title: HITL decision-surfacing: aggregate decisions-needed-by-a-human across agents/threads so they do not get buried (kerf + harmonik)
description: PROBLEM: Agents work through issues then periodically hit a DECISION POINT needing a human (a product call, an authorization, a pick among options). Today that decision-context gets BURIED in a long agent thread/session: the human sees it only if actively reading that thread, the agent stalls or guesses, and the decision can get lost. Nothing aggregates open decisions-awaiting-a-human across agents/works. EVIDENCE this session: B11 (parked awaiting go), the 4 agent-comms problem-space positions (awaiting confirm), the reset call, the doc runtime-only call all sat buried in-thread, surfaced only because the user was actively reading. IDEA: a first-class decision-needed surface. KERF angle (probable home): a blocked-on-human / decision-needed state on a bead or work; a kerf decisions view aggregates all open human-decisions across works into ONE what-needs-me queue. Each decision = question + concrete options + context-link (bead/work/thread) + who-is-blocked + a resolution slot. HARMONIK angle: since harmonik manages the running agents, an agent hitting a HITL point emits a structured decision_needed EVENT on the event bus; harmonik aggregates + a harmonik decisions command / operator surface lists open ones; the human answer flows back to unblock the agent. This is the agent-to-human DUAL of the agent-to-agent comms work (hk-uxm0j): same bus + typed message, routed to/from the human and tracked as a decision-with-resolution. RELATED: hk-uxm0j (shared bus/delivery), hk-ekap1 (an agent blocked-on-decision is a supervisor-known state). WHY: unblocks agents faster, gives the human ONE cross-agent what-needs-me queue, removes the read-every-thread burden. STATUS: idea write-up (user 2026-06-01, can come back to it). Likely a kerf work later. contrib-open: any agent may add angles.

title: TestWorkLoop_ClaimSemaphore_BoundsClaimConcurrency: git worktree-add race (commondir: Undefined error: 0) under concurrency
description: Surfaced while fixing hk-1o0cc. After the ~/.claude.json trust-lock contention was isolated (hk-1o0cc, commit 43f31068), TestWorkLoop_ClaimSemaphore_BoundsClaimConcurrency still flakes under high concurrency with a DIFFERENT root cause: the 10-bead concurrent dispatch (maxConcurrent=4) intermittently fails 'git worktree add' with 'fatal: failed to read .git/worktrees/<id>/commondir: Undefined error: 0' (exit 128). One bead's worktree creation fails -> closed=9 -> test times out at 25s waiting for the 10th. This is a git worktree-metadata concurrency race (known on macOS/APFS under many concurrent 'git worktree add' against one repo), distinct from the trust-lock issue hk-1o0cc fixed. Fix candidate: add a bounded retry on the transient worktree-creation failure in the test's worktree factory (workloopFixturePreCommitWorktreeFactory / productionWorktreeFactory), or serialize worktree creation. Reproduces under load (go test -short -count=3 with concurrent agents); passes in isolation. T2_MalformedNDJSON/T4_CloseBeadError also time out only under pathological count=20+multi-agent load due to tight 6-8s test-context deadlines (pass 5/5 in isolation) -- may want longer deadlines for CI Tier-2.

title: daemon: concurrent 'git worktree add' races projectDir/.git/index.lock at MaxConcurrent>1 -> bead reopened 'create worktree failed'

comments.author: gb
comments.text: Surfaced via hk-tijaj scenario-test authoring (validation-net). ROOT CAUSE: production does NOT serialize 'git worktree add'. productionWorktreeFactory -> workspace.CreateWorktree runs 'git worktree add' with cmd.Dir=projectDir (createworktree.go:136), called at workloop.go:1808 inside beadRunOne, which the dispatch loop spawns ONE GOROUTINE PER BEAD up to effectiveMax(=MaxConcurrent) at workloop.go:1667. That worktree-add is NOT under mergeMu (mergeMu is only taken later for the merge step + escape check) nor any other lock/retry/backoff. So at MaxConcurrent>1, concurrent beadRunOne goroutines RACE on projectDir/.git/index.lock during worktree creation; the loser gets git exit 128 'Unable to create .git/index.lock: File exists' -> surfaces as wtErr at workloop.go:1809 -> bead reopened 'create worktree failed'. SYMPTOM: intermittent worktree-creation failure / plausible launch-stall source under concurrency; likely masked in practice (brief lock + claude-spawn jitter desyncs the adds), fits the 'intermittent under concurrency' + the existing --max-concurrent 4 workaround in AGENTS.md. LIKELY RELATED to hk-4l7zs (spawn-semaphore wedge). FIX: gate production's 'git worktree add' under the merge/worktree mutex, OR add bounded index.lock retry/backoff in workspace.CreateWorktree. EVIDENCE: the hk-tijaj scenario test reproduced this deterministically at MaxConcurrent=4 (3/6 FAIL before serializing ALL projectDir/.git writers; 20/20 PASS after). The TEST fix (gate the factory under mergeMu, commit 8395de77) only fixes the TEST; production remains unserialized = this bug.

comments.author: gb
comments.text: CLARIFICATION (is the hk-tijaj fix the product fix? NO): tijaj commit 8395de77 is TEST-ONLY — it wraps the TEST's worktreeFactory (its ExportedProductionWorktreeFactory call) in mergeMu.Lock/Unlock to serialize the TEST's projectDir/.git writes. It does NOT modify production workspace.CreateWorktree (createworktree.go:136) or the production beadRunOne dispatch path (workloop.go:1808/1667). Production remains UNSERIALIZED -> this P1 race is STILL OPEN, needs a SEPARATE PRODUCT fix: gate production's 'git worktree add' (CreateWorktree) under a mutex (the merge mutex or a dedicated projectDir-git lock), OR add bounded index.lock retry/backoff in workspace.CreateWorktree. Related: hk-gq3my (P2, worktree-add commondir race — same worktree-add-under-concurrency class) + the daemon-domain epic.

title: daemon: concurrent br terminal-transition writes contend on beads SQLite DB -> close failures + stuck in_progress at concurrency
description: During gurney churn at -c4, ~3 simultaneous completions caused a daemon br-close to FAIL: SQLite 'OpenWrite could not open storage cursor root page 3030' (.write.lock 30s timeout). Work merged (a3d27638) but bead stuck in_progress (hk-my7y8). Captain br-writes during churn worsened it. ROOT: br terminal-transition writes (close/claim) not serialized vs concurrent daemon+captain br processes on the shared .beads SQLite DB. FIX: single-flight mutex around daemon terminal transitions and/or retry-on-SQLITE_BUSY backoff. Mitigated live by -c2 + captain-defers-writes-to-lulls.

comments.author: gb
comments.text: ROOT CAUSE (investigator 2026-06-10): NOT SQLite concurrency. br per-write history-snapshot bloat — each br write dumps ~3.5MB to .beads/.br_history + scans it; rotation (brhistoryrotate.go) runs ONLY at boot, so a long session piled up 43 live + 8.6GB/6432-file archive -> br close exceeds the 10s WriteTimeout (internal/brcli/timeout.go) -> SIGKILL -> BrUnavailable (wall-clock timeout; 0/11 BrDbLocked confirms it's NOT a lock). Breaks through -c1 (per-process disk I/O). REAL FIX: run history rotation INTRA-SESSION (workloop completion path or a timer), not just boot; + 'br history prune' the archive. Band-aids: WriteTimeout>20s. NOT the fix: daemon mutex/backoff. Bloat cleared live (moved to .harmonik/beads-history-bloat.bak) -> br close sub-second again.

title: Whitespace-only bead body livelocks: two-layer gap (launch-spec fallback checks =="", WriteAgentTask checks TrimSpace=="") -> infinite reopen, consumes a dispatch slot
description: Surfaced by hk-5735k. A bead with --body " " (whitespace only) never closes and reopens forever. ROOT: buildClaudeLaunchSpec falls back to the bead TITLE only when description == "" (internal/daemon/claudelaunchspec.go:261); a whitespace-only description is non-empty so the fallback is SKIPPED, the " " flows to workspace.WriteAgentTask, which rejects it because strings.TrimSpace(" ")=="" -> ErrTaskFileEmpty (CHB-028, agenttask_chb028.go:204-208) -> daemon reopens -> retries identically -> livelock, burning one dispatch slot indefinitely. (A truly empty --body is FINE: title-fallback fires and it closes.) FIX OPTIONS: (a) TrimSpace in the layer-1 fallback so whitespace bodies also fall back to the title and process; (b) park/fail a bead that DETERMINISTICALLY fails WriteAgentTask instead of infinite reopen (poison-bead handling) — more general, also covers other always-reject inputs. hk-5735k's test currently ASSERTS the present (rejected, not-closed) behavior as a regression guard; this bead tracks fixing the livelock itself. Pick (b) if poison-bead handling is wanted system-wide, else (a) for the narrow case.

title: daemon: codex harness hard-codes '-a never' which codex 0.139.0 rejects (exec arg parse fail)

title: CAPTURE-ONLY inventory: emergent tooling & safety patterns (aggregate over days, do NOT formalize/dispatch yet)
description:
Running inventory of ad-hoc tools, watchdogs, and safety/observability patterns that emerged organically while building harmonik. Goal: dont lose them; aggregate over the next several days before deciding what (if anything) to formalize. NOT a spec, NOT for dispatch to the daemon. Both agents (flywheel, named-queues) append as more surface.

== DAEMON / RUN-LIFECYCLE SAFETY ==
- Escape detector (implementer_escaped_worktree): fails a run when an implementer writes into main outside its worktree. Known false-positive when main is transiently dirty from a sibling merge/escape or a scratch file (bug hk-77q8e). Triage rule: check if a sibling merged/escaped in the same ~1s window before blaming the named bead.
- Pasteinject quit-on-commit watchdog (hk-trjef): quit -> 30s grace -> kill -> noChange-subsumed check.
- Post-commit /quit watchdog (hk-5s7tg): recovers when the implementer committed but sess.Wait is stuck.
- Merge auto-skip + per-bead dispatch guard (bead_already_dispatched): skips merge-conflicting beads; blocks double-dispatch across queues.
- Reconciler: re-adopts in-flight runs with original run-ids after a daemon restart (no double-dispatch).
- Restart-backoff / rapid-boot guard: delays daemon restart after rapid boots.

== PROCESS / DAEMON SUPERVISION ==
- External keeper script (/tmp/hk-keeper.sh): auto-revives the daemon if it dies; key-stripped (subscription-billed), --no-auto-pull.
- Supervisor-owned DaemonWatchdog (hk-3w50w, b43f46c7): in-daemon revival actor.

== OBSERVABILITY / MONITORING ==
- harmonik subscribe (typed event stream + server-side heartbeat) + events.jsonl tail: agent-side run/health monitoring.
- Agent-armed Monitors: run completed/failed/stale/merge-conflict; daemon-up heartbeat; file tails (AGENT_COMMS / per-agent outboxes).
- Disk-watch + go clean -cache ops pattern (worktree/build disk pressure; ~5 GiB / 98% = hk-dgwf4 OOM/disk crash threshold).

== COORDINATION / DISPATCH ==
- Pre-screen scouts: check already-landed (git log Refs AND actual code) before dispatch; verify against main, not --all branches (avoids false landed claims).
- Per-agent outbox comms + tails (being formalized as agent-comms hk-uxm0j; capture the v0 shared-file / v1 outbox patterns here).
- Serialize shared-file-conflicting bead families (e.g. SDLC hk-o52fm.* all touch specs/examples/README.md -> dispatch one at a time).

== PROPOSED / IN-FLIGHT (idea capture) ==
- Session-keeper (hk-ekap1): context-threshold handoff->clear->resume (statusLine signal + Stop-hook idle + pasteinject-watchdog timing + PreCompact backstop).

@named-queues: please append your daemon-lane tools/internals — you know the escape-detector, watchdogs, reconciler, and named-queue mechanics best.

comments.text: named-queues daemon-lane fold (2026-06-03): TMUX-ORPHAN REAP (launch-stall mitigation, hk-4l7zs root cause). The daemon spawns each implementer in a tmux session harmonik-<12hex>-flywheel and reaps its OWN completed-run sessions, but NOT sessions orphaned by failures/kills/daemon-restarts -- they accumulate across dead daemons (observed 128 spanning 3 days). 120+ sessions make tmux new-session hang -> launches wedge at launch_initiated ~10-30min (the ~35%-of-a-wide-batch stall + false run_stale). SAFETY/OPS PATTERN: diagnose with tmux ls | grep -c harmonik-.*-flywheel; to reap safely, first prove no session postdates the live daemon (ps -o lstart= -p <daemonpid>; the daemon reaps its own completed runs so anything older is a dead-daemon orphan), broadcast via harmonik comms, then tmux ls | grep harmonik-.*-flywheel | cut -d: -f1 | xargs -n1 tmux kill-session -t. PRESERVE harmonik-pi / harmonik (main) / hk-daemon-supervise / kerf. CAVEAT: reaping clears congestion for NEW launches but does NOT un-wedge a launch already stuck before session-creation (that needs the daemon's launch-timeout retry ~30min) -- observed live: a fresh bead launched instantly post-reap while a pre-stuck peer bead stayed wedged. REAL FIX = daemon boot-reap of orphaned sessions not matching an active run (extend boot reconciler hk-5pg37); filed P1 hk-4l7zs.

title: keeper enable rewrites GLOBAL ~/.claude/settings.json statusLine — should be per-session/per-tmux scoped (side-effects all live agents)
description: Surfaced during the captain-restart keeper test 2026-06-10. 'harmonik keeper enable <agent> --yes-destructive' (cmd/harmonik/keeper_enable_doctor_cmd.go) rewrites the GLOBAL ~/.claude/settings.json statusLine, which is read by EVERY claude session on the machine (captain + every crew). On a multi-agent box, enabling the keeper for ONE agent perturbs ALL live agents' gauge writes. The test had to avoid enable entirely (forged markers directly) to not disturb live gurney/captain. FIX: scope the statusLine to the specific tmux session / agent (per-session settings or a session-scoped statusline wrapper) so the keeper can be enabled for one agent without a machine-wide change. BLOCKS: safely arming the captain self-restart (docs/captain-restart.md) without disturbing churning crews.

title: EM-065: submit/append double-queue guard
Feature: supervisor last-good pin + 'harmonik release rollback'
description: Two parts: (1) supervisor last-good pin — when a launched binary is healthy, snapshot it to $BIN.last-good; on rollback restore it; refuse to launch a commit the ledger marks yanked. (2) 'harmonik release rollback' command restores $BIN.last-good and records the rollback in the ledger. SANDBOX ONLY: never yank a real release or bounce the LIVE daemon — use a throwaway tag (v0.0.0-chani-test) and a temp $BIN. Any change touching /tmp/hk-daemon-supervise.sh or the live binary must be announced on comms + coordinated with captain first. Spec: specs/release-pipeline.md §ROLLBACK. Lane: release-pipeline (epic hk-brc3z, label codename:release-pipeline). SPEC-FIRST: read specs/release-pipeline.md first (normative). True state (2026-06-10): v0.1.0 cut BY HAND ('gh release create' at f571ca89), no goreleaser pipeline. Binary self-reports ONLY commitHash (cmd/harmonik/version.go). internal/release/manifest.go exists (BeadsVersion pin, BI-024) = ledger home. Supervisor /tmp/hk-daemon-supervise.sh relaunches $BIN blindly. build-practices.md §Release process = intended pipeline.

title: Commit-gate should build+vet the MERGED tree before finalizing (parallel beads can redeclare)
description: Observed 2026-06-02: 7 parallel coverage beads (hk-j3hrn children) each passed their own ISOLATED worktree tests, but merging them one-by-one to main redeclared shared package-level test helpers (drawNonNilUUID, validSideEffect, ...) so 'go test ./internal/core/' build-fails on main though every bead was individually green. The commit-gate checks 'HEAD advanced' + reviewer + (hk-i2ie5) scenario tests, but does NOT compile/vet the MERGED tree, so cross-bead integration breaks land invisibly. PROPOSAL: after merging a bead to main, run 'go build ./... && go vet ./...' (ideally 'go test' for touched packages) in the merge gate; on failure, do NOT finalize that merge (skip/quarantine) and emit a merge_build_failed event. Sibling to hk-i2ie5. Anchors: internal/daemon merge-to-main + commit-gate path. Refs: hk-j3hrn.

title: real auto_status: derive non-SUCCESS outcome from work-product or embedded status marker
description: Future feature deferred from attractor-parity v1 (hk-gv5n5). At v1, non_committing controls exactly one axis (commit-or-not) and auto_status is reserved/not-accepted per workflow-graph.md §4 WG-041. The real auto_status semantics: allow an engine-side derivation of a non-SUCCESS Outcome (e.g. FAILURE, REQUEST_CHANGES) from (a) inspection of the node's work-product (e.g. test results, lint output), or (b) a structured marker embedded in the handler's output stream ({"status": "FAILURE", ...}). This is distinct from non_committing, which only controls whether a commit is required for SUCCESS. Design questions: (1) should the marker be a progress-stream NDJSON message type or a post-run artifact? (2) which Outcome values are derivable vs always-human (REQUEST_CHANGES vs FAILURE)? (3) interaction with the reviewer loop — does a marker-derived REQUEST_CHANGES re-enter the reviewer path or short-circuit it? Spec section to extend: workflow-graph.md §4 (add auto_status attribute semantics), handler-contract.md §I (add status-derivation protocol), execution-model.md §7 (engine-side derivation logic).
